_comment: Controls which tools build_dataset.py may draw from and each tool's parameter ranges. `weight` is a relative sampling probability among enabled tools (not required to sum to 1). Each param spec's `kind` is one of: choice (uniform pick from `values`), uniform (rng.uniform(min, max), optionally rounded via `round`), randint (rng.randint(min, max)), fixed (always `value`). A param can carry `only_if: {"param": <name>, "values": [...]}` to only be drawn when an earlier-drawn param (same tool, dict order) matched one of those values -- e.g. denoise's `noise_factor` only applies when `algorithm` came out as `spectral_subtraction`. `clipping`'s `clip_fraction` and every full-segment tool's audio_begin/audio_end are computed by build_dataset.py from the clip's real duration, not drawn from here. Heavy ML tools (remove_target/extract_target/human_voice_enhance/super_resolution) are disabled by default since they need their own conda env (`env`, mirroring gen_2nd_stage_data/disturb_recover.py's TOOL_ENV) -- flip `enabled` once that env is available on this machine.

clip_fraction.description: Fraction of the clip's duration to keep, cut from the start (audio_begin=0).

n_steps.description: Semitones to shift; kept away from 0 so the edit is audible.

rate.description: Stretch factor; >1 speeds up, <1 slows down.
